channel.title: Pasta Grannies
channel.description:
Welcome to Pasta Grannies! I find and film real Italian grannies - nonne - making delicious, traditional, handmade pasta. And sometimes soups, breads, dolci, rice dishes, that kind of thing, because that is what the Grannies wanted to cook for me. I'm saving skills and sharing traditions one recipe and Granny at a time!
Pasta Grannies posts every Friday afternoon.

item.description: Discover a savoury rice pie while listening to some of Giannina's life story. This 'torta di riso alla Genovese' is popular in Genova area of Liguria and cooks all have their own tweaks (some add nutmeg for example). 250 ml milk 180g arborio rice 50ml olive oil, salt, 300g in total of fresh cheese, in this case 250g of Prescinsêua and 25g of Campagnolo, 20g of Parmigiano. Try a mix of yoghurt with live cultures with cottage or feta cheese for a soured flavour instead of these local cheeses. for the dough 300g 00 flour 50ml olive oil Salt Warm water, as needed.
item.title: 91yr old Giannina makes a Ligurian cheesy rice pie! | Pasta Grannies
item.author: Pasta Grannies

item.description: This is called Pasticcio alla Carlofortina, it's from Sardinia, but also has its roots in Liguria. We went to Isola San Pietro to find out more about this recipe. Carolina shares her recipe. For the pasta dough: 500g semola rimacinata (semolina flour) 250ml water, a pinch of salt ( or a mixture of bought pasta) For the ragu: 500g fresh or tinned tuna, 500ml passata, 1 onion, 4 basil leaves, one glass white wine. for the pesto: 1 garlic clove, 5 basil leaves, 3 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. 2 tablespoons of Italian pine nuts. If you wish to find out more about Carolina, her Instagram handle is @ricettedicaro
item.title: Discover a tuna with basil pesto pasta from Sardinia! | Pasta Grannies
item.author: Pasta Grannies

item.description: Cooking is a family activity for Rita and Giovanna and here they are making their favourite mushroom lasagna. For the dough: 300g 00 flour, 3 eggs For the béchamel: 1 litre semi skimmed milk, 80g each of butter and flour, plenty of nutmeg For the mushrooms: a 750g mixture, including porcini if you can, 2 garlic cloves, 1/2 a glass of white wine, olive oil (not too much), salt To assemble: 250g mozzarella, 50g Parmigiano or similar.
item.title: Enjoy Rita, Giovanna and grandson Giulio make mushroom lasagna | Pasta Grannies
item.author: Pasta Grannies

item.description: Culurgiones are beautiful wheat-ear shaped ravioli from Sardinia.Every village in the Ogliastra area has its own version and Ottavia shares hers from Tertanía, south west Sardinia. Enjoy both her pasta and her weaving! Pasta dough: 300g semola/durum wheat flour, 120ml water, 30ml olive oil Filling: 500g potatoes, 100g fresh goats curd, 25g pecorino, 1 tablespoon dried mint, pinch of salt, 1/2 garlic clove, a little flour if necessary, 100ml olive oil fried with 1 garlic clove. Dress with tomato sauce and grated pecorino cheese
item.title: Enjoy 87yr old Ottavia and her potato-filled culurgiones! | Pasta Grannies
item.author: Pasta Grannies

item.description:
These stuffed bread rolls are called nfriulate and they are the Sicilian equivalent to Cornish pasties. They are a speciality of Lecara Friddi which is where Frank Sinatra's grandparents came from and the townsfolk are very proud of both. Here Francesca shares her recipe for what was Sinatra's grandfather Francesco's favourite meal.
For the filling: 500g fresh chard or spinach 500g fresh Italian fennel flavoured sausages (add a teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds if your sausages are plain) 1 onion, 3 large potatoes olive oil, salt, black pepper
bread dough: use your own recipe. Francesca used 500g semola rimacinata (though strong bread flour will also be fine), 1/2 cube of fresh yeast, 10g sugar, 5g salt, around 300ml of water.
item.title: Francesca makes Sinatra's granddad favourite sausage bread roll | Pasta Grannies
item.author: Pasta Grannies
